{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\n\n# Session Workflow\n\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\n\n---\n\n## 1. READ — Load Context\n\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\n\n---\n\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\n\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\n\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\n\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\n\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \"No production environment detected\" in the session log.\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\n\n**If production issues are found:**\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\n\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\n\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\n\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\n\n### 2c. PRD Sync\n\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\n\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\n\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\n\n---\n\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\n\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\n- What you will work on this session and why\n\nSelect work in this priority order:\n\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \"Auto-approving after no objection.\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\n\n---\n\n## 4. ACT — Execute\n\nFor each piece of work:\n1. Implement the change\n2. Run lint, tests, build\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\n\nIf you deployed anything this session:\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\n\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\n\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\n\n---\n\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\n\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\n\n```\nREAD\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\n\nREVIEW\n[ ] Production health checked\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\n\nDECIDE\n[ ] Triage summary written\n[ ] Work selected by priority\n\nACT\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\n\nVERIFY\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\n\nDOCUMENT\n[ ] Session log written\n[ ] Memory updated\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc","uuid":"124a1ab7-3c66-4f58-aef4-f6d8d947fcf5","timestamp":"2026-03-20T19:00:09.052Z","isSynthetic":true}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwmMtQ8vpWWJsH1PkA4a9k","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc","uuid":"30fc3b98-058b-4a5d-b769-8f91a2903e72","timestamp":"2026-03-20T19:00:16.580Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DWo56MLAarJN7CX4hRUbVN","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Agent Memory\n     2→\n     3→## Project\n     4→- **Name**: Bookmate\n     5→- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     6→- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n     7→- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n     8→- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n     9→\n    10→## Current State\n    11→- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n    12→- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n    13→- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n    14→- Build passes clean, lint has 3 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n    15→- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n    16→- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    17→- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n    18→- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n    19→- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n    20→\n    21→## Production\n    22→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    23→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n    24→- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    25→- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n    26→- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    27→- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n    28→- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n    29→- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n    30→\n    31→## Open PRs\n    32→- None\n    33→\n    34→## Closed Recently\n    35→- #41: Health endpoint, custom 404, OG meta tags (merged + deployed)\n    36→- #39: Production deployment (merged)\n    37→\n    38→## Open Issues\n    39→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — replied with deployment URL and webhook instructions\n    40→\n    41→## Important Notes\n    42→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    43→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    44→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    45→- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    46→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n    47→- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n    48→- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n    49→- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n    50→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    51→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    52→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    53→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    54→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    55→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    56→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    57→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    58→- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n    59→- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n    60→- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n    61→- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n    62→\n    63→## Next Session Priority\n    64→1. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n    65→2. Propose domain + HTTPS setup (needs-human for domain registration)\n    66→3. Consider proposing: error monitoring, rate limiting, image optimization\n    67→4. Check production health via /api/health\n    68→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc","uuid":"42201d49-92c7-4c74-b903-2b76973193ef","timestamp":"2026-03-20T19:00:16.577Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint has 3 warnings (img elements — intentional for Open Library covers)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #41: Health endpoint, custom 404, OG meta tags (merged + deployed)\n- #39: Production deployment (merged)\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — replied with deployment URL and webhook instructions\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check issue #21 for human response on Telegram token\n2. Propose domain + HTTPS setup (needs-human for domain registration)\n3. Consider proposing: error monitoring, rate limiting, image optimization\n4. Check production health via /api/health\n","numLines":68,"startLine":1,"totalLines":68}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UGkEkpEtLEvDzhSURVWBZm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    46→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    47→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    48→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    49→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    50→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    51→\n    52→## Working Directory\n    53→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    54→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    55→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    56→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc","uuid":"9f889b33-2cd7-4d79-b06f-d1382de36edd","timestamp":"2026-03-20T19:00:16.588Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":56,"startLine":1,"totalLines":56}}}

   47→{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc\",\"uuid\":\"124a1ab7-3c66-4f58-aef4-f6d8d947fcf5\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-20T19:00:09.052Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n session-workflow\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc\",\"uuid\":\"b4bdd5ac-9a51-4a3c-9f3c-a7c3e0a0b46e\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-20T19:00:09.053Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"success\":true,\"commandName\":\"session-workflow\"}}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc\",\"uuid\":\"124a1ab7-3c66-4f58-aef4-f6d8d947fcf5\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-20T19:00:09.052Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_stop\"},\"session_id\":\"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"97565cd0-bacd-4331-b2b1-074e67bcbf83\"}\n{\"type\":\"rate_limit_event\",\"rate_limit_info\":{\"status\":\"allowed\",\"resetsAt\":1774044000,\"rateLimitType\":\"five_hour\",\"overageStatus\":\"rejected\",\"overageDisabledReason\":\"out_of_credits\",\"isUsingOverage\":false},\"uuid\":\"a1ed9e54-4f61-42b9-99c4-a1f20311317d\",\"session_id\":\"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc\"}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_start\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-opus-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01J4b2GxASKUy25fNYHgvbZ8\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1641,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13766,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1641},\"output_tokens\":9,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}}},\"session_id\":\"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"4094044f-27df-4408-9c74-32300d9df253\"}","numLines":50,"startLine":1,"totalLines":403}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Gh5aFHaBxV7bvJ9MQMCpRS","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Production looks healthy - the homepage loads with listings showing. Let me close the browser and proceed.\n\nNow let me assess:\n1. Production: ✅ Healthy, homepage loads fine\n2. Issue #21: Still needs-human, last comment was from us about deployment. Human hasn't responded since. This is now many sessions stale. I should add a follow-up comment.\n3. PRD Sync: All features have issues, all closed. No new issues needed.\n4. No open PRs.\n\n**DECIDE**: What to work on?\n\nAll approved issues are done. No P0/P1/P2 open. I should propose improvements. Let me think about what would be most valuable:\n\n\nSince all PRD work is complete, I'm considering several areas where I could add value - UX polish, loading states and error handling, responsive design, lazy loading for book covers, accessibility, SEO optimization, and performance improvements like caching. Let me dig into the codebase to identify the most impactful improvements to propose.\n\nThe app's core value is helping users find and manage book reading groups, so I should focus on enhancing the browse and listing detail experiences. I'm thinking about tackling loading skeletons for better perceived performance, clearer empty states when filters return no results, and a thorough responsive design audit to ensure mobile usability works smoothly.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":390,"cache_read_input_tokens":37430,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":390},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc","uuid":"80e1df5a-5f38-43be-adf4-4bebc142a6b9"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UHvd1n61KfRmG3S171HvDD","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Listing {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  book_title: string;\n     9→  book_author: string;\n    10→  book_cover_url: string;\n    11→  language: string;\n    12→  reading_pace: string;\n    13→  start_date: string;\n    14→  meeting_format: string;\n    15→  max_group_size: number;\n    16→  requires_approval: number;\n    17→  platform_preference: string;\n    18→  member_count: number;\n    19→  author_name: string;\n    20→  created_at: string;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→export default function HomePage() {\n    24→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    25→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    26→\n    27→  // Search & filter state\n    28→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    29→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    30→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    33→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    34→\n    35→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    36→\n    37→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    38→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string) => {\n    39→      setLoading(true);\n    40→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    41→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    42→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    43→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    44→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    45→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    46→\n    47→      const qs = params.toString();\n    48→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    49→        .then((r) => r.json())\n    50→        .then((data) => {\n    51→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    52→          setLoading(false);\n    53→        })\n    54→        .catch(() => setLoading(false));\n    55→    },\n    56→    []\n    57→  );\n    58→\n    59→  // Initial fetch\n    60→  useEffect(() => {\n    61→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\");\n    62→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    63→\n    64→  // Debounced search\n    65→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    66→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    67→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    68→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    69→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n    70→    }, 400);\n    71→  };\n    72→\n    73→  // Immediate filter changes\n    74→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    75→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    76→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n    77→  };\n    78→\n    79→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n    80→    setReadingPace(value);\n    81→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom);\n    82→  };\n    83→\n    84→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n    85→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n    86→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value);\n    87→    93→\n    94→  const clearFilters = () => {\n    95→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n    96→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n    97→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n    98→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n    99→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   100→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\");\n   101→  };\n   102→\n   103→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   104→\n   105→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   106→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   107→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   108→ className=\"text-center mb-8 mt-4\">\n   117→        <h1 className=\"text-4xl mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   118→          Find your reading companion\n   119→        </h1>\n \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   125→          }}\n   126→        >\n   127→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   128→          connect on Telegram to read together.\n   129→        </p>\n   130→      </div>\n   131→\n   132→      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n   133→      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n   134→        <div className=\"flex gap-3 author...\"\n   139→              value={searchQuery}\n   140→              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n   141→              className=\"input-field\"\n   142→ \"next/link\";\n     4→import { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\n     5→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     6→\n     7→interface User {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  email: string;\n    10→  displayName: string;\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→export default function Navbar() {\n    14→  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null);\n    15→  const [notifCount, setNotifCount] = useState(0);\n    16→  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n    17→  const router = useRouter();\n    18→\n    19→  useEffect(() => {\n    20→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    21→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    22→      .then((data) => {\n    23→        if (data.user) {\n    24→          setUser(data.user);\n    25→          setNotifCount(data.user.unreadNotificationCount || 0);\n    26→        }\n    27→      })\n    28→      .catch(() => {\n    29→        // Auth check failed — stay logged out\n    30→      });\n    31→  }, []);\n    32→\n    33→  const handleLogout = async () => {\n    34→    await fetch(\"/api/auth/logout\", { method: \"POST\" });\n    35→    setUser(null);\n    36→    setMenuOpen(false);\n    37→    router.push(\"/\");\n    38→    router.refresh();\n    39→  };\n    40→\n    41→  return (\n    42→    <nav\n    43→      className=\"border-b sticky top-0 z-50\"\n    44→      style={{\n    45→        borderColor: \"var(--color-border)\",\n    46→        backgroundColor: \"var(--color-bg)\",\n    47→      }}\n    48→    >\n    49→      <div className=\"max-w-5xl mx-auto px-4 h-16 flex items-center justify-between\">\n    50→        <Link\n    51→          href=\"/\"\n    52→          className=\"text-xl font-bold\"\n    53→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-accent)\", letterSpacing: \"-0.03em\" }}\n    54→        >\n    55→          bookmate\n    56→        </Link>\n    57→\n    58→        <div className=\"flex items-center gap-4\">\n    59→          {user ? (\n    60→            <>\n    61→              <Link\n    62→                href=\"/listings/create\"\n    63→                className=\"btn-primary text-sm\"\n    64→                style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 1rem\" }}\n    65→              >\n    66→                + New Listing\n    67→              </Link>\n    68→\n    69→              <div className=\"relative\">\n    70→                <button\n    71→                  onClick={() => setMenuOpen(!menuOpen)}\n    72→                  className=\"flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer\"\n    73→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif\" }}\n    74→                >\n    75→                  <div\n    76→                    className=\"w-8 h-8 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-bold text-white\"\n    77→                    style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-accent)\" }}\n    78→                  >\n    79→                    {user.displayName.charAt(0).toUpperCase()}\n    80→                  </div>\n    81→                  {notifCount > 0 && (\n    82→                    <span\n    83→                      className=\"badge text-white\"\n    84→                      style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-error)\", fontSize: \"0.65rem\" }}\n    85→                    >\n    86→                      {notifCount}\n    87→                    </span>\n    88→                  )}\n    89→                </button>\n    90→\n    91→                {menuOpen && (\n    92→                  <div\n    93→                    className=\"absolute right-0 top-12 w-48 card shadow-lg py-2 px-0\"\n    94→                    style={{ padding: 0 }}\n    95→                  >\n    96→                    <div\n    97→                      className=\"px-4 py-2 text-sm font-bold truncate\"\n    98→                      style={{\n    99→                        borderBottom: \"1px solid var(--color-border)\",\n   100→                        fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   101→                      }}\n   102→                    >\n   103→                      {user.displayName}\n   104→                    </div>\n   105→                    <Link\n   106→                      href=\"/profile\"\n   107→                      className=\"block px-4 py-2 text-sm hover:bg-gray-50\"\n   108→                      onClick={() => setMenuOpen(false)}\n   109→                      style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   110→                    >\n   111→                      Profile\n   112→                    </Link>\n   113→                    <Link\n   114→                      href=\"/notifications\"\n   115→                      className=\"block px-4 py-2 text-sm hover:bg-gray-50\"\n   116→                      onClick={() => setMenuOpen(false)}\n   117→                      style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   118→                    >\n   119→                      Notifications\n   120→                      {notifCount > 0 && (\n   121→                        <span\n   122→                          className=\"ml-2 badge text-white\"\n   123→                          style={{\n   124→                            backgroundColor: \"var(--color-error)\",\n   125→                            fontSize: \"0.6rem\",\n   126→                          }}\n   127→                        >\n   128→                          {notifCount}\n   129→                        </span>\n   130→                      )}\n   131→ cursor-pointer\"\n   135→                      style={{\n   136→                        borderTop: \"1px solid var(--color-border)\",\n   137→                        fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   138→                        color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n   139→                      }}\n   140→                    >\n   141→                      Sign out\n   142→ items-center gap-3\">\n   149→              <Link\n   150→                href=\"/auth/login\"\n   151→                className=\"btn-secondary text-sm\"\n   152→                style={{ padding: {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HmDy9M65a4QQKt7oLfe63z","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→\n     5→interface BookResult {\n     6→  key: string;\n     7→  title: string;\n     8→  author_name?: string[];\n     9→  cover_i?: number;\n    10→}\n    11→\n    12→interface Props {\n    13→  onSelect: (book: {\n    14→    title: string;\n    15→    author: string;\n    16→    coverUrl: string;\n    17→    olid: string;\n    18→  }) => void;\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→export default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n    22→  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    23→  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n    24→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    25→  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n    26→  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    27→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  useEffect(() => {\n    30→    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    31→      if (\n    32→        containerRef.current &&\n    33→        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n    34→      ) {\n    35→        setOpen(false);\n    36→      }\n    37→    };\n    38→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    39→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    40→  }, []);\n    41→\n    42→  useEffect(() => {\n    43→    if (query.length < 3) {\n    44→      setResults([]);\n    45→      return;\n    46→    }\n    47→\n    48→    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    49→\n    50→    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n    51→      setLoading(true);\n    52→      try {\n    53→        const res = await fetch(\n    54→          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`\n    55→        );\n    56→        const data = await res.json();\n    57→        setResults(data.docs || []);\n    58→        setOpen(true);\n    59→      } catch {\n    60→        setResults([]);\n    61→      } finally {\n    62→        setLoading(false);\n    63→      }\n    64→    }, 400);\n    65→\n    66→    return () => {\n    67→      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    68→    };\n    69→  }, [query]);\n    70→\n    71→  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    72→    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    73→    onSelect({\n    74→      title: book.title,\n    75→      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n    76→      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n    77→        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n    78→        : \"\",\n    79→      olid,\n    80→    });\n    81→    setQuery(book.title);\n    82→    setOpen(false);\n    83→  };\n    84→\n    85→  return (\n    86→    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n    87→      <input\n    88→        type=\"text\"\n    89→        className=\"input-field\"\n    90→        value={query}\n    91→        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n    92→        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n    93→        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n    94→      />\n    95→      {loading && (\n    96→        <div\n    97→          className=\"absolute right-3 top-3 text-xs\"\n    98→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    99→        >\n   100→          Searching...\n   101→        </div>\n   102→      )}\n   103→\n   104→      {open && results.length > 0 && (\n   105→        <div\n   106→          className=\"absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 card shadow-lg max-h-72 overflow-y-auto\"\n   107→          style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 0\" }}\n   108→        >\n   109→          {results.map((book) => (\n   110→            <button\n   111→              key={book.key}\n   112→              type=\"button\"\n   113→              onClick={() => handleSelect(book)}\n   114→              className=\"w-full text-left px-4 py-2 hover:bg-gray-50 flex items-center gap-3 cursor-pointer\"\n   115→            >\n   116→              {book.cover_i ? (\n   117→                <img\n   118→                  src={`https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-S.jpg`}\n   119→                  alt=\"\"\n   120→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 object-cover rounded\"\n   121→                />\n   122→              ) : (\n   123→                <div\n   124→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm\"\n   125→                  style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\" }}\n   126→                >\n   127→                  📖\n   128→                </div>\n   129→              )}\n   130→              <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n   131→                <div\n   132→                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold truncate\"\n   133→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   134→                >\n   135→                  {book.title}\n   136→                </div>\n   137→                <div\n   138→                  className=\"text-xs truncate\"\n   139→                  style={{\n   140→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   141→                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   142→                  }}\n   143→                >\n   144→                  {book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\"}\n   145→                </div>\n   146→              </div>\n   147→            </button>\n   148→          ))}\n   149→        </div>\n   150→      )}\n   151→    </div>\n   152→  );\n   153→}\n   154→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018rgjeXzBWyzVHEUgKeTWfX","session_id":"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc","uuid":"f75a866f-2808-4e8f-b612-66166662e2cf","timestamp":"2026-03-20T19:02:28.535Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AVQW6LiD3w34r4APmU6EEK","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import BookSearch from \"@/components/BookSearch\";\n     6→\n     7→export default function CreateListingPage() {\n     8→  const router = useRouter();\n     9→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    10→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    11→\n    12→  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = useState(\"\");\n    13→  const [bookAuthor, setBookAuthor] = useState(\"\");\n    14→  const [bookCoverUrl, setBookCoverUrl] = useState(\"\");\n    15→  const [bookOlid, setBookOlid] = useState(\"\");\n    16→  const [language, setLanguage] = useState(\"English\");\n    17→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    18→  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState(\"\");\n    19→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"text\");\n    20→  const [maxGroupSize, setMaxGroupSize] = useState(\"4\");\n    21→  const [requiresApproval, setRequiresApproval] = useState(false);\n    22→  const [platformPreference, setPlatformPreference] = useState(\"telegram\");\n    23→\n    24→  const handleBookSelect = (book: {\n    25→    title: string;\n    26→    author: string;\n    27→    coverUrl: string;\n    28→    olid: string;\n    29→  }) => {\n    30→    setBookTitle(book.title);\n    31→    setBookAuthor(book.author);\n    32→    setBookCoverUrl(book.coverUrl);\n    33→    setBookOlid(book.olid);\n    34→  };\n    35→\n    36→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    37→    e.preventDefault();\n    38→    setError(\"\");\n    39→    setLoading(true);\n    40→\n    41→    try {\n    42→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/listings/create\", {\n    43→        method: \"POST\",\n    44→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    45→        body: JSON.stringify({\n    46→          bookTitle,\n    47→          bookAuthor,\n    48→          bookCoverUrl,\n    49→          bookOlid,\n    50→          language,\n    51→          readingPace,\n    52→          startDate,\n    53→          meetingFormat,\n    54→          maxGroupSize,\n    55→          requiresApproval,\n    56→          platformPreference,\n    57→        }),\n    58→      });\n    59→\n    60→      const data = await res.json();\n    61→\n    62→      if (!res.ok) {\n    63→        if (res.status === 401) {\n    64→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    65→          return;\n    66→        }\n    67→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to create listing\");\n    68→        return;\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      router.push(`/listings/${data.id}`);\n    72→    } catch {\n    73→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n    74→    } finally {\n    75→      setLoading(false);\n    76→    }\n    77→  };\n    78→\n    79→  return (\n    80→    <div className=\"max-w-lg mx-auto\">\n    81→      <h1 className=\"text-3xl mb-2\">Start a reading group</h1>\n    82→      <p\n    83→        className=\"mb-8\"\n    84→        style={{\n    85→          color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    86→          fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    87→          fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n    88→        }}\n    89→      >\n    90→        Choose a book, set your pace, and wait for readers to join.\n    91→      </p>\n    92→\n    93→      {error && (\n    94→        <div\n    95→          className=\"mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg text-sm\"\n    96→          style={{\n    97→            backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n    98→            color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n    99→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   100→          }}\n   101→        >\n   102→          {error}\n   103→        </div>\n   104→      )}\n   105→\n   106→      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-5\">\n   107→        <div>\n   108→          <label\n   109→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   110→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   111→          >\n   112→            Book\n   113→          </label>\n   114→          <BookSearch onSelect={handleBookSelect} />\n   115→          {bookTitle && (\n   116→            <div\n   117→              className=\"mt-2 flex items-center gap-3 p-3 rounded-lg\"\n   118→              style={{ backgroundColor: \"rgba(224, 122, 58, 0.06)\" }}\n   119→            >\n   120→              {bookCoverUrl && (\n   121→                <img\n   122→                  src={bookCoverUrl}\n   123→                  alt=\"\"\n   124→                  className=\"w-10 h-14 object-cover rounded\"\n   125→                />\n   126→              )}\n   127→              <div>\n   128→                <div\n   129→                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold\"\n   130→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   131→                >\n   132→                  {bookTitle}\n   133→                </div>\n   134→                <div\n   135→                  className=\"text-xs\"\n   136→                  style={{\n   137→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   138→                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   139→                  }}\n   140→                >\n   141→                  by {bookAuthor}\n   142→                </div>\n   143→              </div>\n   144→            </div>\n   145→          )}\n   146→        </div>\n   147→\n   148→        <div>\n   149→          <label\n   150→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   151→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   152→          >\n   153→            Discussion language\n   154→          </label>\n   155→          <select\n   156→            className=\"input-field\"\n   157→            value={language}\n   158→            onChange={(e) => setLanguage(e.target.value)}\n   159→          >\n   160→            <option>English</option>\n   161→            <option>Spanish</option>\n   162→            <option>French</option>\n   163→            <option>German</option>\n   164→            <option>Russian</option>\n   165→            <option>Portuguese</option>\n   166→            <option>Italian</option>\n   167→            <option>Japanese</option>\n   168→            <option>Chinese</option>\n   169→            <option>Korean</option>\n   170→            <option>Other</option>\n   171→          </select>\n   172→        </div>\n   173→\n   174→        <div>\n   175→          <label\n   176→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   177→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   178→          >\n   179→            Reading pace\n   180→          </label>\n   181→          <input\n   182→            type=\"text\"\n   183→            className=\"input-field\"\n   184→            value={readingPace}\n   185→            onChange={(e) => setReadingPace(e.target.value)}\n   186→            required\n   187→            placeholder='e.g. \"1 chapter per week\" or \"50 pages per day\"'\n   188→          />\n   189→        </div>\n   190→\n   191→        <div>\n   192→          <label\n   193→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   194→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   195→          >\n   196→            Start date\n   197→          </label>\n   198→          <input\n   199→            type=\"date\"\n   200→            className=\"input-field\"\n   201→            value={startDate}\n   202→            onChange={(e) => setStartDate(e.target.value)}\n   203→            required\n   204→          />\n   205→        </div>\n   206→\n   207→        <div>\n   208→          <label\n   209→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   210→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   211→          >\n   212→            Meeting format\n   213→          </label>\n   214→          <div className=\"flex gap-3\">\n   215→            {[\n   216→              { value: \"text\", label: \"Text chat\" },\n   217→              { value: \"voice\", label: \"Voice calls\" },\n   218→              { value: \"mixed\", label: \"Mixed\" },\n   219→            ].map((opt) => (\n   220→              <button\n   221→                key={opt.value}\n   222→                type=\"button\"\n   223→                onClick={() => setMeetingFormat(opt.value)}\n   224→                className=\"flex-1 py-2 px-3 rounded-lg text-sm font-semibold transition-colors cursor-pointer\"\n   225→                style={{\n   226→                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   227→                  backgroundColor:\n   228→                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n   229→                      ? \"var(--color-accent)\"\n   230→                      : \"transparent\",\n   231→                  color:\n   232→                    meetingFormat === opt.value\n   233→                      ? \"white\"\n   234→                      : {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018G36KUFe2oMzoBuwwLX2UZ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { Metadata } from \"next\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import ListingDetail from \"./ListingDetail\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ListingRow {\n     6→  book_title: string;\n     7→  book_author: string;\n     8→  book_cover_url: string;\n     9→  reading_pace: string;\n    10→  author_name: string;\n    11→  max_group_size: number;\n    12→}\n    13→\n    14→export async function generateMetadata({\n    15→  params,\n    16→}: {\n    17→  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;\n    18→}): Promise<Metadata> {\n    19→  const { id } = await params;\n    20→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    21→\n    22→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    23→    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  const listing = db\n    27→    .prepare(\n    28→      `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n    29→              l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n    30→       FROM listings l\n    31→       JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    32→       WHERE l.id = ?`\n    33→    )\n    34→    .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing) {\n    37→    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n    41→  const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n    42→\n    43→  return {\n    44→    title,\n    45→    description,\n    46→    openGraph: {\n    47→      title,\n    48→      description,\n    49→      type: \"article\",\n    50→      ...(listing.book_cover_url\n    51→        ? {\n    52→            images: [\n    53→              {\n    54→                url: listing.book_cover_url,\n    55→                alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n    56→              },\n    57→            ],\n    58→          }\n    59→        : {}),\n    60→    },\n    61→    twitter: {\n    62→      card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n    63→      title,\n    64→      description,\n    65→      ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    66→    },\n    67→  };\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→export default function ListingPage() {\n    71→  return <ListingDetail />;\n    72→}\n    73→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018rgjeXzBWyzVHEUgKeTWfX","session_id":"e6d3f2af-a613-4d81-ba9e-8bae882444bc","uuid":"02b30008-7ac1-4fe6-b3d6-91a1a3d516c3","timestamp":"2026-03-20T19:02:30.969Z"}
